semanticCore: Snatching, seizing, stealing a glance, flash, or kidnap
quranicRelevance: Birds snatching bodies (Takhtafu - 'fatakhtafuhu at-tayru'), snatching by lightning ('yakadu al-barqu yakhtafu'), and snatching a quick hearing ('illa man khatifa').

keyDerivations.meaning: To snatch, seize rapidly, take away, flash, or steal glance
To snatch away, sweep away, carry off, kidnap, or eradicate
Rapid snatch, quick glance, or swift seizure

figurativeOrRareUsages: إِلَّا مَنْ خَطِفَ الْخَطْفَةَ فَأَتْبَعَهُ شِهَابٌ ثَاقِبٌ (37:10): Except he who snatches a rapid hearing/glance (of celestial counsel), and is pursued by a piercing flame.
summaryEn: The root represents rapid snatching, describing cosmic devils snatching celestial hints (Khatfa), lightning snatching sight, and birds snatching unguided souls.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الاختطاف والانتزاع السريع، واصفاً استراق الشياطين للسمع (خطف الخطفة)، وخطف البرق للأبصار، وتخطف الطير للشرك.
rawText: أَخْطَفُ الحَشَا : see what next follows. اختطف 8 ; and its variations خَطَّفَ and خِطِّفَ; and يِخِطِّفُ and يَخِطِّفُ, variations of its aor.: see 1, in seven places. === كَأَنَّهُ يَخْتَطِفُ فِى مَشْيِهِ عُنُقَهُ, said of a swift camel, means As though he were straining, or stretching, (يَجْتَذِبُ,) in his going along, his neck. (S.) ___ See also 4. اخطف بِالأَمْرِ 4 He said, Seize thou this [thing], O man; or take it, or carry it off, by force; or snatch it away. (Sgh.) ___ أَخْطَفَ لِى مِنْ حَدِيثِهِ شَيْئًا ثُمَّ سَكَتَ, inf. n. إِخْطَافٌ, † He cut short somewhat of his discourse, or narrative, which he had begun to me, on some other thing's occurring to his mind, and was silent. (TA.) === أَخْطَفَتْهُ الحُمَّى, (Lh, O, TA,) or أَخْطَفَتْ عَنْهُ, (JK,) or === اِخْتَطَفَتْهُ, (K,) † The fever left him, or quitted him. (Lh, JK, O, K.) === اخطفهُ المَوْتُ † [Death missed him by a little;] he escaped death by a little. (JK.) And اخطف الرَّمِيَّةَ † He missed the animal at which he shot or cast, (JK, S, K,) nearly hitting it: (JK:) and in like manner, الشَّىْءَ the thing. (Ibn-Buzurj, TA.) And He captured, or caught, the animal at which he shot or cast; expl. by إِذَا كَانَ يَصِيدُهَا [perhaps a mistranscription for يُصِيبُهَا, and, if so, meaning he hit]. (JK.) And اخطف said of an arrow, † [It missed: or it fell upon the ground, and then glided along upon the ground to the butt, or object of aim: (see خَاطِفٌ:) and] it went straight. (TA.) === اخطف said of a man, † He became affected with a slight sickness, and then speedily recovered. (TA.) === أِخْطَافُ الحَشَا i. q. اِنْطِوَآؤُهُ [meaning † The state of being lean, or lank, in the belly: see مُخْطَفٌ]. (S, TA.) الأِخْطَافُ in horses is a fault: it is † The contr. of الاِنْتِفَاخُ: AHeyth says that it is, in horses, † smallness of the جَوْف [here meaning the belly, or abdomen]. (TA.) الخَطَّافُ [lit. He that is wont to seize, &c.: and particularly † he that is wont to snatch, or steal, opportunities of hearing the speech of the angels, from the confines of the lowest Heaven: and hence.] applied in a trad. to † the Devil, or Satan: (S, TA:) or, as some say, it is in this instance 🡻الخُطَّافُ, as pl. of خَاطِفٌ, [and therefore meaning † the devils,] or as being likened to the hooked iron called خُطَّاف. (TA.) === أَبُو الخَطَّافِ a surname of The حِدَأَة [or kite]. (TA in art. حدأ.) تخاطفوا الكُرَةَ بَيْنَهُمْ 6 [They contended together in snatching away the ball] with the goffsticks. (K * and TA in art. جحف.) تَخَطَّفَ 5 see 1, in two places. خَاطُوفٌ A thing like a reaping-hook, which is tied to a snare, and by which the gazelle is caught. (JK, O, L, K.) خَاطِفٌ [act. part. n. of 1, Seizing, &c.]: pl. خُطَّافٌ. (TA.) === الخَاطِفُ The wolf; (JK, S, K;) because he seizes, or carries off by force, his prey. (TA.) === خَاطِفُ ظِلِّهِ A certain bird, (JK, S, K,) said by Ibn-Selemeh to be called الرَّفْرَافُ; (S, [so in three copies, not رَقْرَاق as in Freytag's Lex.,] TA;) that sees its shadow, and thinks it to be a bird; (JK;) or when it sees its shadow in the water, it advances to it to seize it, (S, L, K,) thinking it to be a prey: (L, TA:) [see خَيَالٌ:] it is one of the birds of the deserts, and is [said to be] thus called because of the swiftness with which it pounces down; it is green, or of a dark, or an ashy, dust-colour, (أَخْضَرُ,) in the back; white in the belly; long in the wings, and short in the neck: (Msb in art. لعب:) also called مُلَاعِبُ ظِلِّهِ. (S and Msb in that art.) === بَرْقٌ خَاطِفٌ ‡ Lightning that takes away the sight. (JK, S, * TA. *) === سَهْمٌ خَاطِفٌ † An arrow that falls upon the ground, and then glides along upon the ground to the butt, or object of aim; as though snatching something from the ground: pl. خَوَاطِفُ: (Ham p. 573:) or خَوَاطِفُ signifies arrows that miss; for مُخْطِفَاتٌ. (TA.) خَطَفَى † Quickness in pace or going, (S, K,) of a camel, as though he were straining, or stretching, his neck, in going along; (S; [see 8;]) as also 🡻خَيْطَفَى, (K,) and 🡻خَيْطَفٌ. (JK, TA.) === See also the last of these words below. خَطِفَهُ 1 , aor. ﹷ, (S, Mgh, Msb, K,) inf. n. خَطْفٌ; (S, TA;) this is the approved form of the verb; (T, S;) and خَطَفَهُ, aor. ﹻ, (S, Msb, K,) inf. n. as above; (Msb;) a form of the verb mentioned by Akh, (S,) but this is rare, (S, K,) or (K) bad, (S, K,) scarcely, or not at all, known; (S;) and 🡻اختطفهُ, (S, Mgh, Msb, TA,) and 🡻تخطّفهُ; (S Msb, TA;) He seized it; or took it, or carried it off, by force: (S, K:) or he did so quickly; snatched it away: (Mgh, Msb, TA:) and 🡻خطّف has been said to imply repetition of the action [unless it be a variation of اختطف as in a case mentioned below]; but this is strange, and not known on any other authority than that of the “ Akáneem et-Taaleem ” by El-Khuweiyee, a disciple of El-Fakhr Er-Rázee. (MF, TA.) Hence, in the Kur [xxix. 67], 🡻وَيُتَخَطَّفُالنَّاسُ مِنْ حَوْلِهِمْ [And men are carried off by force from around them]. (TA.) === [And hence,] هٰذَا سَيْفٌ يَخْطَفُ الرَّأْسَ ‡ [This is a sword that will strike off the head]. (TA.) === And خَطِفَ البَصَرَ and خَطَفَهُ, said of lightning, (K,) and of a ray of light, and of a [glistening] sword, and of any polished body, (TA,) ‡ It took away the sight: (K, TA:) and ↓اُخْتُطِفَ بَصَرُهُ ‡ His sight was suddenly taken away. (M and K in art. ملس.) It is said in the Kur [ii. 19], يَكَادُ البَرْقُ يَخْطَفُ أَبْصَارَهُمْ ‡ [The lightning almost taketh away their sight, lit. sights]: (TA:) Yoo read يَخْطِفُ ابصارهم; (S, TA;) and so did Aboo-Rejà and Mujáhid: and some read ↓يِخِطِّفُ, and ↓يَخَطِّفُ, originally يَخْتَطِفُ, accord. to the opinion of the Basrees, disputed by Fr, but confirmed by Zj. (TA.) === And خَطِفَ السَّمْعَ, (K,) aor. ﹷ, (S,) said of a devil, ‡ He stole [an opportunity of] hearing [the speech of the angels, from the confines of the lowest Heaven; or snatched it]; (S, K, TA;) as also ↓اختطفهُ: (K:) the two verbs being like نَزَعَهُ and اِنْتَزَعَهُ. (Sb, TA.) Hence, in the Kur [xxxvii. 10], ↓إِلَّا مَنْ خَطِفَ الخَطْفَةَ ‡ Except him who steals the [opportunity of] hearing: (TA:) or who snatches unawares and by stealth, (Bd,) or hears and snatches, (Jel,) the speech of the angels: (Bd, Jel:) EL-Hasan read الّا من الخطفة ↓خَطَّفَ, originally ٱخْتَطَفَ: (S, TA:) and another reading, ascribed to him and others, is ↓خِطِّفَ; but this is very weak. (TA.) === خَطِفَ, aor. ﹷ; and خَطَفَ, aor. ﹻ; inf. n. خَطَفَانٌ; (K;) thus in all the copies of the K, but correctly خَطْفٌ, as in the L; (TA;) said of a camel, † He went along quickly. (K, TA.) And مَرَّ يَخْطَفُ خَطْفًا مُنْكَرًا † He went along at a quick rate [such as was deemed strange, or disapproved]. (TA.) And خَطِفَتِ السَّفِينَةُ, and خَطَفَت, † The ship sailed, or voyaged: you say, خَطِفَتِ اليَوْمَ مِنْ عُمَانَ † She sailed,. or voyaged, to-day, from 'Omán. (TA.) خَطِيفٌ : see خَيْطَفٌ. خَطِيفَةٌ The act of seizing, or carrying off by force; or, of snatching away at unawares. (TA.) ___ Flour sprinkled upon milk, (S,) or flour upon which milk is sprinkled, (JK, K,) then cooked, (JK, S, K,) and licked, or eaten with the finger, (S, K,) and snatched up with spoons: (K:) IAar says that it is [what is called] جَبُولَآءُ [a word I have not found in any other in
